Digital Teacher Portfolio

Building biliteracy through connection.

I am a bilingual Spanish educator and Single Subject Spanish Credential candidate who designs culturally sustaining instruction that helps secondary students build confidence, language proficiency, and authentic communication skills.

Biliteracy Authorization SDSU Credential Candidate Comprehensible Input / TPRS Secondary Spanish

About my practice

Language learning should feel human.

My teaching is rooted in relationships, meaningful input, clear routines, and respect for students' linguistic and cultural identities. I want students to experience Spanish as a living language connected to identity, family, culture, and community.

Instruction

Comprehensible and purposeful

I use visuals, repeated structures, storytelling, sentence frames, partner practice, and formative checks so students can understand, respond, and grow in Spanish.

Biliteracy

Culture as an asset

I value students' languages, home knowledge, and cultural experiences as strengths that belong in the classroom and support language development.

Community

Families as partners

I value bilingual communication, family connection, and authentic opportunities for students to use Spanish beyond the classroom.

Portfolio artifacts

Required materials, ready to review.

This digital portfolio includes the required teacher portfolio contents in one continuous-scroll website. Select any artifact to preview the document. All required materials are embedded directly in this portfolio.

Instructional practice

From input to expression.

My sample lesson asks students to understand a familiar Spanish story, practice oral retelling with a partner, and produce a written shrinking summary that captures the main idea and key details.

Comprehension

Students use visuals, vocabulary support, repeated language, and story context to interpret meaning before producing language.

Expression

Students move from partner-supported oral retell to independent written summary, building confidence with Spanish structures.

Evidence of readiness

A clear focus: students, language, and belonging.

My teaching experience and recommendations highlight a student-centered approach grounded in comprehensible input, professionalism, and inclusive classroom relationships.

Current experience

Mira Mesa High School

I plan and deliver standards-aligned Spanish lessons, assess student learning, support classroom routines, and collaborate with my mentor teacher to support diverse learners.

Workshop design

Vamos a Platicar

I designed a Spanish conversation workshop focused on authentic language use, real-world contexts, and community connections.

Prior teaching

Teaching in Mexico

My seven years of teaching experience strengthened my ability to adapt instruction and connect with students across linguistic and cultural backgrounds.
